entities.summary: Triton kernel storing K/V vectors into paged cache blocks

detail.name: store_kvcache_kernel
detail.description: GPU kernel writing computed K/V pairs into pre-allocated cache blocks at correct positions.
detail.why: Custom kernel avoids Python overhead for the hottest operation in inference

teaches.explain: Write GPU kernels in Python with Triton. Compiles to PTX. Easier than CUDA C but same performance for memory-bound ops.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Custom kernel avoids Python overhead for the hottest operation in inference

teaches.explain: Store K/V at block_table[block_idx][position]. Indirection enables non-contiguous memory without copying.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Custom kernel avoids Python overhead for the hottest operation in inference

detail.perf: Called every token for every layer — must be maximally optimized

entities.summary: Python wrapper launching the Triton KV cache store kernel

detail.name: store_kvcache
detail.description: Sets up grid dimensions and launches the Triton kernel with correct tensor pointers.

teaches.explain: Python sets grid/block dimensions and tensor pointers. Triton/CUDA kernel runs on GPU. Separation of orchestration from computation.
teaches.rationale: Sets up grid dimensions and launches the Triton kernel with correct tensor pointers

entities.summary: Multi-head attention with paged KV cache and flash attention

detail.name: Attention
detail.description: Computes scaled dot-product attention using cached K/V from paged memory. Uses flash attention for efficiency.
detail.why: Attention is the core operation in transformers — must handle variable-length cached KV efficiently

teaches.explain: Tiled attention algorithm that never materializes full N×N attention matrix. O(N) memory instead of O(N²). Enabled by online softmax.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Attention is the core operation in transformers — must handle variable-length cached KV efficiently
teaches.cross_lang: Hardware-specific: CUDA (FlashAttention), Metal (MLX attention), no direct CPU equivalent
teaches.gotcha: Requires specific GPU architectures (Ampere+). Custom attention masks may not be supported

teaches.explain: K/V vectors stored in fixed-size blocks with indirection table. Avoids memory fragmentation for variable-length sequences.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Attention is the core operation in transformers — must handle variable-length cached KV efficiently

detail.design: Prefill uses flash_attn (variable length); decode uses custom kernel (single query)

entities.summary: Configure attention heads, scaling factor, and cache parameters

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Sets num_heads, head_dim, scale=1/sqrt(d), and prepares for paged KV cache access.

teaches.explain: Divide QK^T by sqrt(head_dim) to prevent softmax saturation. Without scaling, dot products grow with dimension causing gradient vanishing.
teaches.rationale: Sets num_heads, head_dim, scale=1/sqrt(d), and prepares for paged KV cache access

entities.summary: Execute attention: store KV, compute QK^T, apply softmax, multiply V

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Writes new K/V to cache, then dispatches to flash_attn (prefill) or paged_attention (decode).

teaches.explain: Prefill processes all prompt tokens at once (parallel). Decode generates one token at a time (sequential). Different kernels optimal for each.
teaches.rationale: Writes new K/V to cache, then dispatches to flash_attn (prefill) or paged_attention (decode)
